value.market_read: WTI Curve: WTI settled at $74.14 in the prior session, lifted on the back of the WPSR for the week ending June 12 (released Wed Jun 17). The reported crude draw and continued tightening of Cushing balances pulled the prompt higher, with domestic tightness underpinning the front of the curve. The structure is firmly backwardated, with the M1–M12 spread at $5.26, signaling the market is pricing near-term supply tightness rather than a storage surplus. The Aug–Sep prompt spread of roughly $0.82 in backwardation confirms a tight prompt, with deferred contracts sagging toward the high-$60s by mid-2027. Watch: Whether Cushing continues to draw into July, which would steepen front-end backwardation further. Brent Curve: Brent settled at $78.14 in the prior session, holding a $4.00 premium to WTI. The global benchmark moved in sympathy with WTI post-WPSR but the curve shape diverges modestly, with a small dip and recovery across the autumn contracts before resuming a backwardated decline. The M1–M12 spread of $4.99 is slightly flatter than WTI's, indicating prompt tightness is marginally more acute on the domestic side than internationally. The Aug–Sep spread of about $0.39 shows softer prompt urgency than WTI. Watch: Hormuz risk premium and any OPEC+ supply signals that could re-steepen the international curve. RBOB Gasoline Curve: RBOB settled at $2.8924 in the prior session, reflecting peak summer driving demand pulling the prompt sharply higher. The steep backwardation — M1–M12 at roughly 40.7 c/gal — captures the seasonal premium for prompt summer-grade barrels that collapses once demand fades. The curve drops hard from August through the autumn before stabilizing and ticking back up in the spring 2027 contracts as the next driving season is priced in. The Aug–Sep spread near 12 c/gal underscores intense prompt summer pull. Watch: Refinery run rates and the pace of the seasonal demand peak unwinding into post-Labor Day. ULSD Diesel Curve: ULSD settled at $3.0705 in the prior session, with the prompt firm on tight distillate balances. The curve is backwardated but more gently than gasoline, with M1–M12 at roughly 32.1 c/gal, reflecting steady industrial and transport demand rather than a sharp seasonal spike. Unlike gasoline, the distillate curve declines smoothly into 2027 without a spring recovery kink, consistent with the absence of a summer demand inflection. The Aug–Sep spread near 3.2 c/gal points to modest prompt tightness. Watch: Distillate inventory trajectory and any early heating-season positioning into autumn.
value.kpi_wti: WTI settled at $74.14 in the prior session, supported by the latest WPSR draw and tightening Cushing balances driving prompt strength.
value.kpi_brent: Brent settled at $78.14 in the prior session, holding a $4.00 premium to WTI and tracking the post-WPSR move.
value.kpi_rbob: RBOB settled at $2.8924 in the prior session, with steep prompt backwardation reflecting peak summer gasoline demand.
value.kpi_ulsd: ULSD settled at $3.0705 in the prior session, firm on tight distillate balances and steady industrial demand.
value.kpi_bw: The Brent–WTI spread at $4.00 is narrow, signaling weak/uneconomic US export arb — barrels stay domestic and pressure WTI lower until the spread re-widens.
value.kpi_curve_regime: Both crude benchmarks are in firm backwardation (WTI M1–M12 at $5.26, Brent at $4.99), pricing near-term supply tightness rather than a storage surplus.
